multiplechoiceresponse.label: Which three explanation approaches are described as crucial for making black-box AI models more interpretable?

choice.div: Automated hyperparameter tuning, visualization of training loss, and dropout regularization.
Symbolic logic reasoning, handcrafted rule bases, and expert systems.
Surrogate models that mimic black boxes, local explanations such as LIME, and counterfactual what-if scenarios.
User surveys, interactive dashboards, and crowdsourced annotations.

div.p: Black-box models like deep neural networks require post-hoc interpretability methods. These include surrogate models (e.g., decision trees that mimic the black box), local explanations (such as LIME, explaining individual predictions), and counterfactuals (what-if changes that would alter the outcome). Together, they provide diverse ways to make AI more transparent and trustworthy.
